item.title: Steve Jobs - The Man Who Saved Apple Documentary
item.author: The People Profiles
item.content:encoded:
Discover the unvarnished story of Steve Jobs, the visionary entrepreneur who revolutionized the world with Apple but often destroyed the people around him. This comprehensive Steve Jobs biography goes far beyond the standard autobiography praise to reveal the complex 'monster genius' behind the tech giant. From the garage days with Steve Wozniak and the revolutionary launch of the Macintosh to his exile and triumphant return, we uncover the Apple history that shaped Silicon Valley.
While Jobs is famously known for his inspiration and motivational speeches like the Stanford commencement, this documentary exposes the gritty reality. We investigate the controversial Steve Jobs facts—his ruthless management, the rude outbursts, and the exposed moments that painted him as a tyrant. Was his abrasive style necessary for entrepreneurship success, or a tragic flaw?
Join The People Profiles for a definitive look at the man who invented the future. This isn't just a tech story; it's a deep dive into the human cost of greatness. Watch this Steve Jobs documentary to decide for yourself: was he a genius, a monster, or both?

item.title: Historical Tyrants 1: Leopold II of Belgium | European History
item.author: Extra History
item.content:encoded:
📚 When you're done learning about the Historical Tyrant Leopold II of Belgium, head on over to Nebula and get 50% off https://go.nebula.tv/extrahistory , see Extra History episodes 1 week early, watch them without ads, and get exclusive content!
Welcome to Tyrant Scene Investigation, where we examine how power is gained, used, and remembered by history’s most infamous rulers. In this episode, we investigate Leopold II of Belgium, a constitutional monarch at home who became an absolute tyrant abroad as the personal ruler of the Congo Free State. Remembered domestically as the “Builder King,” Leopold engineered a brutal colonial system built on forced labor, rubber extraction, corporate partnerships, and propaganda, resulting in the deaths of millions of Congolese people, and what early human rights advocates would later call crimes against humanity.

item.title: Dictators, drugs, discord - Latin America and the USA (3/3) | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
To this day, the USA has a hand in almost all the crises affecting Latin America. Colombia, for example, sank into chaos in the 1990s. The cartels took over the country. People fled to the United States. Today, President Trump exploits this situation, using it to boost his popularity.
In Venezuela, Hugo Chávez’s victory in the 1998 presidential election sent shockwaves through the country. His radical reforms met resistance from Venezuela’s economic elites, who subsequently organised a coup. But in a dramatic turn of events, Chávez returned to power just 48 hours later. After his return, he blamed the United States for the attempted coup. In the 1990s, Colombia intensified its fight against drug trafficking with the help of the United States. The country was on the brink of collapse, as drug cartels and FARC guerrillas put increasing pressure on the government. With massive US support — and despite numerous human rights violations — President Álvaro Uribe implemented "Plan Colombia”, which was intended primarily to crack down on drug traffickers. But the drug routes simply shifted: Honduras became a new hub for cocaine. Because of ongoing human rights abuses, many Colombians left their country for the United States. Donald Trump exploited the resulting migration crisis, making it a central theme of his 2015 election campaign. Meanwhile, China stepped up its influence in Latin America. Panama’s President Juan Carlos Varela negotiated an agreement with Beijing without informing Washington, a long-standing ally.
Part 1: https://youtu.be/UkZASVWnHmU Part 2: https://youtu.be/IJ3zOE9V18I Part 3: https://youtu.be/tweCup3tL04

item.title: Dictators, drugs, discord - Latin America and the USA (2/3) | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
Nearly all of Latin America’s crises have been shaped by the United States. In the 1980s, the US supported anti-communist forces in Nicaragua and the controversial strongman Manuel Noriega in Panama.
In the early 1980s, Panama avoided a civil war, but a shadowy figure came to power: Manuel Noriega, a long-time CIA agent and protégé of the United States. His main opponent, guerrilla leader Hugo Spadafora, accused Noriega of drug trafficking. In 1988, the US Department of Justice in Florida brought charges against Noriega, including for his links to Pablo Escobar's Medellín cartel. Noriega refused to step down, which led to the US invasion of Panama and his arrest. In Nicaragua, the Sandinistas introduced sweeping reforms after coming to power in 1979. Fearing the spread of a socialist revolution in Central America, the United States armed counter-revolutionary militias known as the Contras. A brutal guerrilla war followed. In Chile, ruler Augusto Pinochet faced growing opposition. In a 1988 referendum, the United States supported the democratic opposition through its ambassador, Harry Barnes. Pinochet was subsequently forced to step down. But another crisis was already unfolding: in 1989, the economic crisis in Venezuela, a traditional ally of the United States, reached its peak. Unrest broke out and was violently suppressed by the army.

item.title: Dictators, drugs, discord - Latin America and the USA (1/3) | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
Almost all Latin American crises of the 1960s and 1970s were influenced by the USA. Whether it was Brazil's dictatorship, the coup in Chile or the Cuban Missile Crisis - at some level, American politics were involved.
Brazil's first "national security" dictatorship was established in 1964 with the support of the United States. Washington, fearing an expansion of a perceived communist threat following the Cuban Revolution, rallied behind the coup leaders - making Brazil the first "national security" dictatorship in the region. Many opponents of the regime fled to Chile, where Augusto Pinochet seized power in a coup - also with the support of the United States. Almost simultaneously, the secret services of Chile, Brazil, Argentina, Bolivia, Uruguay and Paraguay joined forces under the code name "Operation Condor" to track down and eliminate their opponents. But in Nicaragua, a bloody civil war broke out and brought the leftist Sandinistas to power. This gave rise to a period of hope across the continent for greater independence from the United States. But Ronald Reagan's inauguration in 1981 marked a new era in relations with Latin America.
Part 1: https://youtu.be/UkZASVWnHmU Part 2: https://youtu.be/IJ3zOE9V18I Part 3: https://youtu.be/tweCup3tL04

item.title: Who are the Most Extreme Sharks? | MEGA Episode | Nat Geo Animals
item.author: Nat Geo Animals
item.content:encoded:
Travel to different oceans around the world to explore the world's most extreme sharks in this MEGA episode. Featuring three, full episodes:
Atlantic Atlantic sharks exhibit radical variety and have adapted to fit into every part of this complex underworld. Steve encounters a diverse world of sharks in the Atlantic.
Indian The Indian Ocean is teeming with life, but this ocean is changing. Steve sets out to see if these perfect predators can adapt to our rapidly changing world.
Pacific The Pacific Ocean covers an area greater than all land on earth, but it’s under threat. Steve joins scientists to find shark hot spots and how to preserve them.
Most Extreme Sharks | S1, E1

item.title: This Is How You Get JARHEAD Sequels
item.author: Folding Ideas
item.content:encoded:
Clickbait Title: Annihilation 2: Absolution (2027) Ending Explained
I started work on this a year ago. The original script ended with a joke about touching grass, where I needed to go out into the snow and dig a little hole to find some grass to touch, and I even shot it as part of my "get hyped about the project" process. But then the Silkposting and CoD: Ghosts panels were approved for PAX East, so I had to pivot to working on those, and then I promised the final Ghosts video to PAX West as a live premiere, so Jarhead got kicked even further down the road, and then I got invited to a tour of Mr. Beast's studio which I absolutely couldn't turn down because I just could not figure out how they even got my name, so 2025 was a weird year.
Written by Dan Olson and Nathan Landel Edited by Dan Olson and Nathan Landel Performed by Dan Olson Executive Assistant Crystal Donovan
Special Thanks to Big Joel and my Patrons

item.author: FRONTLINE PBS | Official

He previously served as assistant U.S. attorney for the District of Maryland. The following interview was conducted by the Kirk Documentary Group's Mike Wiser for FRONTLINE on April 3, 2025. It has been annotated and edited for accuracy and clarity as part of an editorial and legal review. See a more complete description of our process here.

item.title: The Shocking Transformation Of Russia | Compilation
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
Russia has an incredibly rich history - Czars, Totalitarian Governments, etc etc. Today we go through many aspects of the history of Russia - From Peter the Great to such modern situations as life in the URSSR, the Soviet that saved the world from nuclear annihilation, and what happened when the Soviet Union Collapsed. Wild rollercoaster ride indeed.
Chapters: 00:00:00 - Ernest Hemingway Was the Worst KGB Spy Ever 09:53:21 - How a Soviet Soldier Saved the World From Annihilation 00:20:22 - What It Was Like to Be Held In a Soviet Gulag 00:33:01 - What It's Like To Live In The Diomedes 00:44:14 - The Enduring Mystery Of Rasputin, Russia's Secret Shadow Master 00:56:08 - Fascinating Facts About The Russian Revolution 01:07:49 - Unconventional Foods People Ate In Soviet Russia 01:20:02 - What Actually Happened Right After The Soviet Union Collapsed 01:33:05 - Peter The Great, The Physically Enormous Czar Who Modernized Russia 01:44:07 - The Kursk | What Happened to the Russian Sub That Exploded 01:54:27 - What Life in the Soviet Union Was Like

item.title: Tut's Treasures: Hidden Secrets | MEGA Episode | National Geographic
item.author: National Geographic
item.content:encoded:
Who was King Tut? Delve into ancient, Egyptian history in this MEGA episode of Tut's Treasures: Hidden Secrets. Featuring three full episodes:
Tales from the Tomb Of all the 5,398 treasures from the tomb of Tutankhamun now being assembled and forensically investigated at the Grand Egyptian Museum in Cairo, it’s the ones that paint a picture of Tutankhamun’s family that are the most unsettling.
Treasures Rediscovered Many have never been seen before but together they shed new light on the short, eventful life of the so-called ‘Boy King’ and are now helping experts realise the sheer scale of Tutankhamun’s influence in the ancient world.
Golden Mask King Tut’s incredible death mask – 24lbs of pure gold - has always been the subject of debate with many scholars believing its damaged nameplate, known as a cartouche, and different colours of gold meant it was never made specifically for him. It was more likely made for his female ancestor, Nefertiti.
Tut's Treasures: Hidden Secrets | S1

item.title: Java Generics and Collections • Maurice Naftalin & Stuart Marks
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This interview was recorded for the GOTO Book Club. http://gotopia.tech/bookclub
Check out more here: https://gotopia.tech/episodes/406
Maurice Naftalin - Architect, Trainer & Co-Author of "Java Generics & Collections" Stuart Marks - Java & OpenJDK at Oracle & Technical Editor of "Java Generics & Collections"
RESOURCES Maurice https://bsky.app/profile/mauricenaftalin.bsky.social https://x.com/mauricenaftalin https://github.com/MauriceNaftalin https://www.linkedin.com/in/maurice-naftalin
Stuart https://bsky.app/profile/smarks.bsky.social https://mastodon.social/@stuartmarks https://x.com/stuartmarks https://github.com/stuart-marks https://www.linkedin.com/in/stuart-marks-17a71a2 https://stuartmarks.wordpress.com
DESCRIPTION Maurice Naftalin and Stuart Marks discuss the second edition of "Java Generics and Collections", published 19 years after the original. The conversation explores how Java programming has evolved from Java 5 to Java 25, covering major shifts like the move toward immutability, the introduction of sequenced collections, streams, and unmodifiable collections.
They delve into critical design topics including encapsulation of collections, the anemic domain model anti-pattern, the controversial unsupported operation exception, and the challenges of null handling. The book includes new chapters on usage guidance and design retrospectives that reflect decades of accumulated wisdom about the Collections Framework.
RECOMMENDED BOOKS Maurice Naftalin & Philip Wadler • Java Generics and Collections 2nd ed • https://amzn.to/47dOp9t Maurice Naftalin & Philip Wadler • Java Generics and Collections 1st ed • https://amzn.to/42JI03i Joshua Bloch • Effective Java • https://amzn.to/4oFbdoi Victor Grazi & Jeanne Boyarsky • Real-World Java • https://amzn.to/4oCEeBR Kevlin Henney & Trisha Gee • 97 Things Every Java Programmer Should Know • https://amzn.to/3kiTwJJ Christian Clausen • Five Lines of Code • https://amzn.to/3s2zjyg Nicolai Parlog • The Java Module System • https://amzn.to/3xFggR4

item.title: Why One Oil Company Has a Head Start in Venezuela
item.author: Bloomberg Originals
item.content:encoded:
With President Nicolás Maduro deposed, President Donald Trump wants US oil giants to revive Venezuela’s energy industry. Though it’s unclear who, if anyone, will take part in his plan, Chevron is already there.
After the US attack on the South American country and its rendition of President Nicolás Maduro, Trump said he wants to rebuild the country’s beleaguered infrastructure. While the oil majors mull his entreaties, Chevron may have a distinct advantage since it’s been operating in Venezuela for decades.

item.title: JD Vance Defends ICE Agent Who Shot Minneapolis Woman
item.author: The Wall Street Journal
item.content:encoded:
Vice President JD Vance said that the ICE agent responsible for Wednesday’s fatal shooting of a woman in Minnesota was doing his job as a federal officer and is protected by “absolute immunity.” Vance, during a lengthy appearance in the White House briefing room on Thursday, defended the Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer and criticized the woman who was killed, Renee Nicole Good, calling her a “victim of left-wing ideology.”
The Department of Homeland Security said the agent fired in self-defense after Good attempted to run over officers, but state and city officials have disputed that version of events after videos on social media raised questions about whether Good was attempting to flee the scene rather than harm anyone.
Some officials in ICE’s headquarters were alarmed that the leadership in the Trump administration quickly labeled the incident an act of “domestic terrorism,” rather than waiting to conduct an investigation into the incident, according to people familiar with the matter. Officials internally passed around an interview with border czar Tom Homan on CBS News, in which he said he would reserve judgment while an investigation was under way, the people said.
#Minneosta #JDVance #WSJ

item.title: Upstairs, downstairs: The life of a British maid - Stephanie Honchell Smith
item.author: TED-Ed
item.content:encoded:
Follow Alice Sutton, a servant in Edwardian England, as she prepares the estate for a visitor and fights for women's suffrage.
--
It’s just before dawn in 1906 at Wroxton Abbey, and 16-year-old Alice Sutton’s mind is already spinning. The underhousemaid has a full day ahead of her as the estate prepares for the evening’s guest: Prince Arthur. This is Alice’s chance to prove herself to her new employers — but there are other forces at play. Stephanie Honchell Smith details a day in the life of a servant in Edwardian England.
Lesson by Stephanie Honchell Smith, directed by Anton Bogaty.

item.title: The impact of industrial fishing in the Azores | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
In the Azores, an autonomous region in Portugal, tuna fishing is done the way it has been for generations: one fisherman, one rod, one fish at a time. Nets are banned. Every catch is monitored and recorded.
But despite their efforts to keep fishing as selective and sustainable as possible, the Azores fishermen are struggling to make ends meet. Because of overfishing, the EU imposed fishing quotas that could run them out of business.
Local fishermen say they are frustrated because it’s not their fault the quotas have to be implemented. They say waters are depleted thanks to industrial fishing fleets casting out gigantic purse seine nets further out at sea, where they can easily evade monitoring.
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs #sustainablefishing #overfishing ______

item.title: Hacking AI Systems: How to (Still) Trick Artificial Intelligence • Katharine Jarmul • GOTO 2025
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This presentation was recorded at GOTO Copenhagen 2025. #GOTOcon #GOTOcph https://gotocph.com
Katharine Jarmul - Building Privacy-first Data & AI/ML Systems, Products & Platforms
RESOURCES https://twitter.com/kjam https://linkedin.com/in/katharinejarmul https://github.com/kjam https://kjamistan.com https://probablyprivate.com
ABSTRACT How easy is it to fool or trick today's AI systems?
In this talk, we'll wander through the field of adversarial AI/ML, looking at how attacks and AI systems have evolved over the past 10 years. You'll learn more about how deep learning works by investigating how, when and why it breaks and walk away with open security questions and some notebooks to keep learning and hacking! [...]

item.title: Louis XIV - The Sun King of France Documentary
item.author: The People Profiles
item.content:encoded:
Discover the definitive story of Louis XIV, the monarch who defined an era, in this comprehensive Louis XIV documentary. Known to history as the Sun King, his 72-year rule stands as the longest reign in history and the ultimate symbol of Absolute Monarchy in Europe.
This biography explores how Louis transformed a humble hunting lodge into the magnificent Palace of Versailles, creating a gilded cage for the French nobility and establishing France as the dominant global power. From the chaos of the Frondes to the War of the Spanish Succession, we examine the military campaigns, court scandals, and political intrigues of the Bourbon Dynasty.
Was he a divinely appointed leader or a tyrant who paved the way for revolution? Witness the rise of the man who declared "L'État, c'est moi" (I am the State) and became a titan of French history. Join The People Profiles as we uncover the life of the King who tried to rule the world.

item.title: 3 Important Lessons from A Slow Year of Photography...
item.author: Kyle McDougall
item.content:encoded:
Head to https://squarespace.com/kylemcdougall to save 10% off your first purchase of a website or domain using code KYLEMCDOUGALL
2025 was a year of photography that didn't go as planned. There were a lot of things that I wanted to do, that I did very little of. As a result, I learned, or was reminded of a few important lessons that have become my focus moving forward, and have also inspired some recent changes I've made to my photography approach and business, including moving into a new studio space. In this video, I'm going to talk about those three lessons in hopes that they maybe help you out with your work in one way or another. I'll also give you a quick tour of the new studio!

item.title: The Electronic SA80: The EIW97 (Part Two) with firearms expert Jonathan Ferguson
item.author: Royal Armouries
item.content:encoded:
In this second part of What Is This Weapon, Jonathan and Murray return to the Electronic Individual Weapon 97 (EIW 97), an experimental SA80-based system developed in the 1990s to explore electronic fire control, programmable burst modes, and future soldier integration.
Following on from Episode 1, this episode focuses on how the weapon was programmed, tested, and demonstrated, using period-correct software, RS232 interfaces, and modern adapter hardware to bring the system back to life today.
Watch Episode 1 first for the historical background and mechanical overview.
00:03 Intro 01:04 Programming the weapon: trial laptop, software & RS232 interface 04:50 Adaptive fire control & tailoring rate of fire to the soldier 07:42 Power, sensors & internal diagnostics explained 10:04 Live software demonstration & weapon data readout 15:01 Burst modes, rate-of-fire settings & trial configurations 20:58 Demonstration setup: electronic blank firing attachment 31:00 Live demonstration of electronic firing modes 38:27 Service concerns, reliability & the ‘battery question’ 42:25 Future soldier systems, C4I & integrated weapons 46:44 Why electronic fire control still matters today 57:44 Final thoughts & preservation at the Royal Armouries
#RoyalArmouries #SA80 #L85A1 #L85A2 #L85A3 #SmallArms #BritishArmy #FirearmsHistory #WhatIsThisWeapon
Subscribe to our channel for more videos about arms and armour
Help us bring history to life by supporting us here: https://royalarmouries.org/support-us/donations/
Sign up to our museum membership scheme here: https://royalarmouries.org/support-us/membership/
⚔Website: https://royalarmouries.org/home ⚔Blog: https://royalarmouries.org/stories/ ⚔Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/RoyalArmouriesMuseum/ ⚔Twitter: https://twitter.com/Royal_Armouries ⚔ Instagram: http://instagram.com/royalarmouriesmuseum
We are the Royal Armouries, the United Kingdom's national collection of arms and armour. Discover what goes on behind the scenes and watch our collection come to life. See combat demonstrations, experience jousting and meet our experts.
Have a question about arms and armour? Feel free to leave us a comment and we'll do our best to answer it.

item.title: We've Finally Seen How Planets Form
item.author: Astrum
item.content:encoded:
This is HOPS-315. The James Webb Space Telescope has peered inside a cocoon of gas and dust, revealing a spectacular event for the first time ever. We’re witnessing the birth of a solar system not unlike our own, with proto-planets forming in real-time. Could this discovery finally unlock the secrets of our own solar system’s origin?
▀▀▀▀▀▀
0:00 How Solar Systems Are Born 5:13 How Do Planets Form? 6:26 HOPS-315 8:44 Baby Planets 9:46 Breaking the Models 12:24 What’s Next For HOPS-315?
▀▀▀▀▀▀

item.title: Why Are There No Holes Around Trees?
item.author: Kurzgesagt – In a Nutshell
item.content:encoded:
Go to https://brilliant.org/nutshell/ to dive deeper into these topics and more for free for a full 30 days + get 20% off the premium subscription! This video was sponsored by Brilliant. Thanks a lot for the support!
Get your LIMITED EDITION Golden Duck Pin here https://shop.kgs.link/golden and join us in celebrating 25 million subscribers! Thank you to each and every one of you for making this happen ❤️
Sources & further reading: https://sites.google.com/view/sources-treesii/
Trees are among the most fascinating life forms on Earth, but what makes them truly fascinating is hiding underground! They are able to break solid rocks with hydraulic roots, all while working together with the underground fungal networks that can stretch for kilometers. Above and below, trees are running biological empires we’re only beginning to understand.
How does something so massive survive with roots growing so shallow? What are trees really made of and how are they able to grow so big?
